decision.archive_summary: Active topic remains Codex minor GC regression/guard tuning for Chinese multi-topic detours. Completed work before the latest '继续': added a harder fixed regression case for natural Chinese multi-turn topic switching/return, tightened the packaged guard so it cannot override a shadow-guard veto, reran automatable evals successfully, and then applied two broader fixes: reject activation when `continue` would demote needed raw turns to pin-only, and stop forcing `summary-first` when a lighter raw-keep plan exists. Durable preference: avoid whitelist-like hardcoding; prefer generalizable logic.
decision.reasoning_summary: This is the same active task, so `continue` fits. The next turn mainly depends on the recent implementation state around generalized fixes and successful rerun, not the earliest resolved status snapshots or the brief test-expectation clarification. I preserved the anti-whitelist preference and the successful automated rerun as semantic pins, while evicting older solved raw turns that are now summarized by later turns.
